en.extract:
The necklaced barbet (Psilopogon auricularis) is an Asian barbet species occurring in Laos and southern Vietnam, where it inhabits subtropical, lowland tropical moist forests and montane forests up to an altitude of 2,700 m (8,900 ft).
Cyanops franklinii auricularis was the scientific name proposed by Herbert C. Robinson and C. Boden Kloss in 1919 for a barbet collected at the Langbian Plateau in southern Vietnam.

The '''necklaced barbet''' ('''''Psilopogon auricularis''''') is an [[Megalaimidae|Asian barbet]] species occurring in [[Laos]] and southern [[Vietnam]], where it inhabits [[subtropical]], lowland [[tropical moist forest]]s and [[montane forest]]s up to an altitude of {{convert|2700|m|abbr=on}}.<ref name="iucn status 19 November 2021" />

''Cyanops franklinii auricularis'' was the [[scientific name]] proposed by [[Herbert C. Robinson]] and [[C. Boden Kloss]] in 1919 for a barbet collected at the [[Langbian]] Plateau in southern Vietnam.<ref>{{cite journal |author1=Robinson, H. C. |author2=Kloss, C. B. |year=1919 |title=On Birds from South Annam and Cochin China. Part I. Phasianidæ–Campophagidæ |journal=Ibis |volume=61 |issue=3 |pages=392–453 |doi=10.1111/j.1474-919X.1919.tb02892.x|url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/partpdf/377839 }}</ref>
